metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a content-level AC to child 43c9 verifying that rebar-implement's Phase 4a or 4b section contains the shared-file reference at the oracle-design integration point (e.g. grep for 'shared/test-design.md' within N lines of 'Phase 4' or '4a'/'4b', or an explicit AC naming what language must appear at that point).
findings.finding: Child 43c9's rebar-implement integration has no content-level acceptance criterion — only a grep for the presence of the reference string. The What section specifies substantive integration ('Phase 4a/4b reference the shared file for oracle design (inventory, tier, oracle completeness, gating proof)'), but the ACs only verify that the string 'shared/test-design.md' appears somewhere in the file, not that it appears at the Phase 4a/4b oracle-design point with the specified substantive guidance. This is a scope-narrowing risk under the three-part coverage standard.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'In rebar-implement/SKILL.md, the shared/test-design.md reference appears within the Phase 4a or 4b section (oracle/test-design guidance), not solely in a footnote or notes section — verified by grep or inspection of the integration point.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies held-out test discipline is preserved (e.g., confirm no held-out test file path appears in the context-loading section of any implementation-subagent prompt in the three skills).
findings.finding: The Success Criterion 'The skills' held-out discipline is preserved' has no corresponding Acceptance Criterion that verifies it, leaving a stated success condition unmeasurable.
findings.location: Success Criteria (bullet 2) vs. Acceptance Criteria section

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add {"path": "src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml", "reason": "add R-non-gating-skip, R-fault-wrong-seam, FP-strict-xfail-with-reason, FP-sleep-as-secondary eval cases"} to child e8d3's file_impact.
findings.finding: Child e8d3's file_impact list omits src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml, which the child explicitly states it will modify (both in the What section and in the acceptance criteria).
findings.suggested_fix: Add the eval.yaml entry to file_impact: {"path": "src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml", "reason": "add four new eval cases (2 recall + 2 no-fire) for the two new antipatterns"}.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a verify command to child e8d3 (the child that touches src/rebar/llm/reviewers/) checking: git diff --exit-code -- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E5.md
findings.finding: Parent AC3 requires that 'git diff shows no edit to src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E5.md' — but no child's verify commands or acceptance criteria include an explicit check on this file, leaving the constraint covered only by scope exclusion with no machine-verifiable guard.
findings.location: parent epic AC3 vs. child e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae and child 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 (both lack this check)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child e8d3's verify_commands: {"command": "git diff --exit-code -- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E5.md", "dd_id": "DD4", "dd_text": "plan_review_E5.md not modified"}.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'and the examples README' to AC1's verify scope so the README integration has a covering criterion.
findings.finding: The examples README update is described in the task decomposition for task 43c9 but is absent from AC1, leaving it an orphaned deliverable with no acceptance criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: AC1: 'The Gerrit change adding examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md, the three SKILL.md integrations, and the examples README update is Verified +1 and merged (task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 closed).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan how brainstorm integrates the shared doc given it has no in-skill testing step — either describe a new brainstorm integration point or explicitly scope the SC to 'rebar-debug and rebar-implement, plus brainstorm's plan-review gate reference'.
findings.finding: Context states brainstorm 'treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs' (implying no in-skill testing integration point), yet the Success Criteria requires the shared doc to be loaded by 'all three skills at the point each incorporates testing' — a point that brainstorm does not, by the plan's own account, have.
findings.location: Context paragraph vs. Success Criteria bullet 1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 the explicit disposition of rebar-brainstorm's existing testing prose (remove it, replace it with the shared-doc reference, or explicitly retain it with a reconciliation note) so no contradictory references remain post-merge.
findings.finding: The plan integrates test-design.md into rebar-brainstorm's SKILL.md but does not specify whether rebar-brainstorm's existing testing-related references (currently handled via 'plan-review gate docs') are removed, updated, or left alongside — risking contradictory or stale internal references within that file post-merge.
findings.location: Context section: 'rebar-brainstorm treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs'; Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 scope
findings.suggested_fix: Add to task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900's scope: 'In rebar-brainstorm/SKILL.md, remove or replace any existing testing-via-plan-review-gate prose with a reference to shared/test-design.md; do not leave both side by side.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a structural requirement to task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900: test-design.md must use named sections (e.g., H2 headings) covering each major topic area so it is navigable when embedded in or linked from skill prompts.
findings.finding: The plan creates examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md with substantial content scope (coverage inventory, tier selection, oracle guidance, fault-realism rules, CI gating) but specifies no navigability/structure requirement for the document, risking a dense, unstructured reference that is hard to use from within skill prompts.
findings.suggested_fix: In the task description or AC, add: 'test-design.md must have a top-level section per major topic (coverage inventory, tier selection, oracle guidance, fault-realism, CI gating), each ≤ one screen of prose, with a brief summary line at the top.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a step (to task 43c9 or the AC list) to update `examples/claude-skills/CLAUDE.md` (or equivalent design doc) recording the `shared/` convention — what belongs there, how skills reference it, and why it was introduced — so future skill authors discover and follow the pattern.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `shared/` directory convention under `examples/claude-skills/` — a novel architectural pattern for skill assets — but does not capture this design decision in any CLAUDE.md, ADR, or design-doc update beyond a general README mention, leaving the convention undiscoverable to future skill authors.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: '`examples/claude-skills/CLAUDE.md` (or README) includes a "Shared Assets" section explaining that cross-skill reference material lives in `shared/`, how to link to it from a SKILL.md, and why the pattern exists (DRY test-design guidance).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale clarifying that the `../shared/test-design.md` reference is a prose read-instruction to the executing agent (not a static import) and cite the existing precedent (rebar-debug's `llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md`) as evidence the pattern works at runtime.
findings.finding: The plan states that the '../shared/test-design.md' relative-path reference 'resolves both in this repo and in a skills installation where shared/ sits beside the skill directories', but provides no positive rationale for WHY the reference is expected to work in the Claude Code skills runtime — specifically, it doesn't explain that the reference is a prose instruction to the executing agent (not a static import), so the agent will read the file from the relative path at runtime. The 'mitigation' (README note + dual path citation) is named, but its sufficiency as a mechanism is not justified.
findings.location: Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 description — 'A shared/ directory is used … the portability cost … is mitigated by an explicit README note … each SKILL.md naming the path relative to its own directory (../shared/test-design.md) plus its repo location'
findings.suggested_fix: In the task description or SKILL.md integration spec, add one sentence: 'The reference is a prose instruction — the agent reads the file at the point in the protocol that names it — consistent with how rebar-debug already uses its companion llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md; no static include or symlink mechanism is needed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a fallback instruction in each SKILL.md integration spec for when `../shared/test-design.md` cannot be read at runtime (e.g., 'if the file is not found, proceed with the inline discipline already described in this skill and note the missing reference').
findings.finding: The plan requires each SKILL.md to load `../shared/test-design.md` at runtime (via a file-read instruction to the executing agent), but specifies no fallback behavior for the skill executor if that file is absent — e.g., when a user copies a single skill directory without copying `shared/` alongside it.
findings.location: Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 description — 'Skill integration' section; Success Criteria bullet 1; examples/claude-skills/README.md note
findings.suggested_fix: In each SKILL.md section that loads the shared file, add a one-sentence fallback: 'If `../shared/test-design.md` is not available (e.g. the shared/ directory was not copied alongside this skill), apply the test-design principles stated inline in this skill and note the gap in the session log.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
